not_started.meaning: A local lead/card object exists before image generation has started.

submitted.meaning: The API accepted the run and stored the normalized source image. Avatar generation is waiting for the S3 source-image trigger.
submitted.currentUsage: Set by the API Lambda after source image storage succeeds.

generating.meaning: The card request has been accepted and avatar generation is in progress.
generating.currentUsage: Set while constructing the backend record before generation finishes.

completed.meaning: An avatar provider returned a generated avatar image and the generated/avatar stage has a usable image.
completed.currentUsage: Set when fal.ai or Bedrock image generation succeeds.

fallback_used.meaning: The card is complete and printable, but the final card image uses the normalized uploaded photo because all configured avatar providers failed or were skipped.
fallback_used.currentUsage: Set when the provider priority reaches fallback_original_photo_card.

failed.currentUsage: Reserved for a hard failure path where no fallback card can be returned.

rendering.meaning: The print-composition Lambda is framing the generated avatar and exporting the Canon-ready raster print image.

rendered.meaning: The deterministic print-layout SVG and final Canon SELPHY-ready PNG have been written to S3.

render_failed.meaning: The final framed print asset could not be produced.

not_printed.meaning: No staff print action has been recorded.
not_printed.currentUsage: Default fulfillment state for new Twin Card runs.

printed.meaning: Staff marked the card as printed at the booth.
printed.currentUsage: Set by the Twin Dashboard Cards tab when staff prints a Canon-ready card. Each print action increments printedCount and updates lastPrintedAt.

pending.meaning: The final card is rendered and SES delivery has started.

sent.meaning: SES accepted the customer email with the public Twin Card result link.

failed.meaning: SES delivery was attempted but failed. The DDB row stores emailError for operator review.

skipped.meaning: No customer email was sent because consent, email config, or a valid email contact was missing.

operatorRule: completed and fallback_used mean the generated/avatar stage has a usable image. A card is print-ready only when renderStatus is rendered. Email delivery is tracked separately in emailStatus. failed, render_failed, and email failed require staff attention.
